reasoning: The Lean statement matches the informal claim closely. It takes an arbitrary finite group G with |G| = 351, and asserts the existence of a prime p dividing |G| together with a Sylow p-subgroup P of G that is normal. The conditions `p.Prime` and `p ∣ card G` together correspond exactly to "a prime p dividing its order", and `P.Normal` for `P : Sylow p G` is the standard Mathlib encoding of "a normal Sylow p-subgroup". The quantifier structure (∃ p, ∃ P, prime ∧ divides ∧ normal) aligns with the informal "for some prime p … a normal Sylow p-subgroup". No hidden extra hypotheses or weakenings appear.
